title: Congress Disagrees on Homeland Security Money
summary: In Congress, the Senate passed a plan to end the shutdown for most of the Department of Homeland Security, which helps keep the country safe. But House Speaker Mike Johnson said no because the plan didn't include money for ICE and Border Patrol, who work at the borders. Now, House Republicans want a different plan, which might make the shutdown last longer. Meanwhile, President Trump used an executive order to pay TSA workers at airports, who check bags and people for safety. Some TSA workers have quit because they haven't been paid, and they feel tired of being used in political fights. The shutdown is causing problems like long lines at airports.

keywords.explanation: the group of people in Washington, D.C., who make laws for the United States
when parts of the government stop working because there's no money to pay for them
a big part of the government that works to keep America safe from dangers
people who work at airports to check bags and passengers to make sure everyone is safe
a special rule made by the President to do something without Congress agreeing first
arguments between different groups in government about what to do

questions.correct_answer: Passed a plan to end it

options: It didn't include money for ICE and Border Patrol
It had too much money for TSA workers
questions.correct_answer: It didn't include money for ICE and Border Patrol

What problem is the shutdown causing at airports?

options: Tired of being used as pawns

questions.question: What might happen if House Republicans push a different plan?

background_read: The government shutdown happens when Congress can't agree on how to spend money, so some parts of the government stop working. This affects workers who don't get paid and services people rely on, like airport security. The Department of Homeland Security includes agencies like TSA, ICE, and Border Patrol that protect the country. Political disagreements in Congress can lead to shutdowns, causing problems for everyone.
Article_Structure: WHO: Congress members, House Speaker Mike Johnson, President Trump, TSA workers
WHAT: A disagreement over money for the Department of Homeland Security leading to a shutdown
WHERE: In Washington, D.C., at Congress and airports across the U.S.
WHY: Because the Senate's plan didn't include money for ICE and Border Patrol, and House Republicans want a different approach

perspectives.description: TSA workers are struggling without pay and feel used in political fights, wanting stability and fair treatment.
